    *   PI-MAX ICCD cameras: 512 x 512 pixels, Tamron AF 28-300 mm F3.5/6.3 lenses
    *   LIF emission angles: 85° and 95°
    *   PLIF depth of field: 6 mm
    *   PIV depth of field: 3 mm
    *   Stereo-PIV laser: Blue Sky PIV laser (Nd:YAG laser, 532 nm, 120 mJ/pulse)
    *   TSI PIVCAM 10-30 CCD cameras resolution: 1000 x 1016 pixels
    *   Nikon AF Micro-Nikkor 105 mm

1.  **Mathematical Equations**: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

2.  **Graphs/Charts**:

    *   Figure 2: Timing diagram for simultaneous double-pulsed acetone PLIF and stereoscopic PIV.
    *   Y-axis Labels: Stereoscopic PIV External Trigger, Steroscopic PIV Camera Exposure, Nd:YAG Laser Two Pulses 532 nm, PLIF Laser 1 External trigger, ICCD Camera 1 Exposure, PLIF Laser 2 External trigger, ICCD Camera 2 Exposure, LIF Laser Two Pulses 266nm
    *   Key Values: 30 us, delta t us

3.  **Tables**: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams**: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**:

    *   F/2.8 (lenses)
    *   Al2O3 particle diameter: 0.5 µm
    *   Laser wavelengths: 532 nm, 266 nm
    *   266-nm laser sheet dimensions: 60 x 0.4 mm
    *   532-nm laser sheet dimensions: 70 x 1 mm
    *   PIV laser pulse delay: 30 µs
    *   First 266-nm laser pulse: 15 µs after the first PIV laser pulse.
    *   Optimal firing time for the second PLIF pulse: 300 to 400 µs after the first pulse
    *   Turbulent time scale: 2 ms
    *   PLIF camera intensifier gate width: 1 µs
    *   Burner inlet diameter: 25.4 mm
    *   Burner outlet diameter: 86 mm
    *   Extension portion length: 86 mm
    *   Swirl number: 0.01
    *   Flow rate: 360 L/min
    *   Reynolds number: 19,000.
    *   Reactant velocity: 12 m/s
    *   Turbulence intensity: 20%
    *   Off-center distances for image planes: 7.5 mm, 15 mm
    *   Acetone level: 9% of fuel concentration
    *   ICCD camera pixel area: 0.1 x 0.1 mm
    *   PLIF spatial resolution: 0.2 x 0.2 x 0.4 mm

title: 07_PIV_and_PLIF_turbulent_premixed_flame
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's a breakdown of the technical information extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   pu"c"/rho = -DTnabla(c_tilde)
*   pu"c"/rho = c_tilde (1 - c_tilde) (ū_P - ū_R)

**2. Graphs/Charts**

* **Figure 5:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Horizontal Distance (mm) - Values from approx. -15 to 15
        *   Vertical Distance (mm) - Values from approx. 10 to 40
        *   Vorticity (1/s) - Color Scale varying from -1000 to 1000
    *   Curves:
        *   Flame boundary (black contour)
        *   Turbulent eddies (colored vectors)

* **Figure 6:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Horizontal Distance (mm) - Values from approx. -20 to 20
        *   Vertical Distance (mm) - Values from approx. 15 to 40
        *   Vorticity (1/s) - Color Scale varying from -1000 to 1000 with middle black line close to 0
    *   Curves:
        *   Flame boundary (black contour)
        *   Turbulent eddies (colored vectors)

* **Figure 7:**
    *   Axes:
        *   Curvature (1/mm): values approx. from -3 to 3.
        *   PDF, mm: values from 0 to 0.4
    *   Curve:
        * Probability distribution of flame boundary curvature

**3. Tables**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams**

*   (In Figures 5 and 6, but this is visual and hard to separate from graph description)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Velocity Fluctuations: 2-3 m/s
*   Time Delays (Δt): 0, 300, 400 µs
*   Mean progress variable (c_tilde) at maximum correlation: ~ 0.5
*   Mean progress variable (c_tilde) range for near constant correlation: 0.2 to 0.8
*   Data taken 7.5 mm away from the centerline.

*   **Figure 9 & 10: Out-of-plane velocity distribution**
    *   Axes:
        *   Vertical Distance, mm
        *   Radial Distance, mm
    *   Units:
        *   mm (millimeters)
        *   m/s (meters per second)
    *   Curves: vectors of velocity distribution
    *   Key values: colorbar for W, m/s ranging approximately from -3 to 1.5 in Fig 9 and -3 to 1.5 in Fig 10. 

*   **Figure 11 (A): Temporal correlation of mean progress variable...**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis:  C'(t)C'(t + Δt)
        *   x-axis:  C
    *   Units: No units available on graph
    *   Curves:
        *   Δt=0 µsec
        *   Δt=300 µsec
        *   Δt=400 µsec
    *   Key values: C ranges from 0.1 to 1, C'(t)C'(t + Δt)  values vary for each curve (see OCR)

*   **Figure 11 (B): Normalized correlation of the mean progress variable**

    *   Axes:

        *   y-axis: C'(t)C'(t + Δt)/C²
           
        *   x-axis: C
    *  Units: No units available on graph
           
    *   Curves:

        *   Δt=300 µsec
        *   Δt=400 µsec
    *   Key values: C ranges from 0.1 to 1, C'(t)C'(t + Δt)/C² values vary for each curve (see OCR)

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

*   Measurements taken at two locations: 15mm and 7.5mm from axis

    *   y-axis range: -0.8 to 0.1
*   **Labels on graph:**
    * square: sgn(dc/dx)*u"c"/SL
    * triangle: sgn(dc/dy)*v"c"/SL
    * diamond: sgn(dc/dz)*w"c"/SL

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT
